bullets.text: Hurricane Katrina generated nearly 30,000 claim reports and an expected loss of $185 million — approximately 5.5 times Progressive's prior single highest catastrophe loss; over 90% of claims resolved by end of October.
Combined ratio of 90.4 for Q3 despite Katrina and Rita losses; favorable prior-year reserve development of $93.9 million partially offset catastrophe costs.

Favorable frequency continued broadly; $93.9 million of prior-year reserve development recognized as lower-than-estimated severity on closed and open claims; management emphasized accuracy over labeling development as favorable.
Net premiums written grew 5% for Q3 and 7% year-to-date; year-over-year premium per application down about 5% for Drive and 2% for Direct, reflecting soft market pricing.

Personal Lines PIF up 10% year-to-date (Drive auto 6%, Direct auto 12%, Special Lines 14%); Commercial Auto up 12%; Internet-initiated Direct business share increased another 5 percentage points above prior guidance of 50–55%.
Progressive entered New Jersey on September 30 with both Drive and Progressive Direct brands; planned to add Commercial Auto later in 2005 and motorcycle and boat products in 2006.

Hurricane response deployed over 1,300 claims representatives; 5,000 Katrina flood-contaminated total-loss vehicles ordered crushed rather than returned to market, reflecting strong ethics commitment.
